overview.summary: Binary Search finds elements in a sorted array by repeatedly halving the search window.
learningObjectives: Identify when to apply binary search on abstract search spaces
overview.interviewRelevance: Critical for optimizing O(N) search to O(log N).

paragraphs: Looking up a word in a physical dictionary uses binary search. You don't read page 1, 2, 3.
You open to the middle, decide if your word is earlier or later, and discard half the book instantly.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: In a sorted sequence, knowing you are at the wrong value automatically tells you which half contains the right value.

definition.technicalDefinition: Binary search is a search algorithm that finds the position of a target value within a sorted array by comparing the target to the middle element.

vocabulary.definition: The range of possible valid indices (or values) currently being considered.
A sequence that is entirely non-decreasing or non-increasing. A strict requirement for binary search.
The calculated center index of the current search window.

operations.name: Search Target
operations.description: Standard binary search. Left and right bound the window. Compare target to mid.
operations.visualNote: The unsearchable greyed out area grows logarithmically each step.

practice.title: Search a 2D Matrix
practice.keyInsight: Treat a 2D matrix conceptually as a 1D sorted array.

practice.title: Koko Eating Bananas
practice.keyInsight: Binary searching over the answer space, not an array.

practice.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
practice.keyInsight: Compare mid to the right pointer to find the pivot.

practice.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
practice.keyInsight: Determine which half is properly sorted, then bound the target.

practice.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
practice.keyInsight: Binary search on the smaller array's partitioning line.
